steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence sets up a parallel comparison using "whereas": the combtooth blenny's highest count was 62 individuals in January 2001, so the blank must supply the yellowtail sergeant's actual highest count in the same format — a number and a month taken directly from the graph.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '82 individuals in January of 2001': The yellowtail sergeant never reaches 82 individuals on the graph; 82 is higher than the combtooth blenny's own peak, which contradicts the passage's point that combtooth blenny had the higher count. ✗
  • Choice B — '8 individuals in April of 2001': The count of 8 is plausible, but the graph shows the yellowtail sergeant's peak occurring in January, not April. The month is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — '62 individuals in January of 2001': 62 is the combtooth blenny's count, not the yellowtail sergeant's. Using the same count for both species would eliminate the contrast the sentence is making. ✗
  • Choice D — '8 individuals in January of 2001': The graph shows the yellowtail sergeant's peak at approximately 8 individuals in January 2001 — the correct count at the correct month. This completes the parallel structure and preserves the contrast (62 vs. 8). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
